title: Change of legal status - Canada.ca
content:
## Change of legal status
In general, a change of legal status or ownership type is due to either:
- a change in the number of owners of the business
- a change in the incorporation status
In most cases, you have to close your existing business number (BN) and Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) accounts and register for new ones.

## Changing the number of owners of the business
Your legal status does not change if all of the following are true:
- your business is a partnership
- there is a change in the number of partners (but not less than two)

### In a sole proprietorship
Your legal status must change if all of the following are true:
- your business is not incorporated
- the number of owners of your business changes, for example, from one owner to multiple owners or from multiple owners to one owner
When the number of owners in a sole proprietorship changes, your business legal status changes in one of the following ways:
- from sole proprietorship to partnership
- from partnership to sole proprietorship
For these legal status changes, you have to call the CRA at 1-800-959-5525 .

## Changing whether the business is incorporated or not

### When your business becomes incorporated
Your legal status can change in one of the following ways:
- from sole proprietorship to corporation
- from partnership to corporation
When you change your legal status to incorporate an existing business, you need to do the following:
You must contact the appropriate incorporating authority and register a new corporation, even though you already have an existing BN .
If you are incorporating with Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada or one of the provincial incorporating authorities listed on Canada Revenue Agency registration and provincial or other federal programs , when you need a BN, the following will apply:
- a new BN will automatically be registered with the CRA for your new corporation
- a new corporation income t ax (RC) program account will automatically be associated to the new BN
- skip step 2 and go to step 3
Once your incorporating authority has provided you with your new incorporation information, contact the CRA at 1-800-959-5525 to register a new BN . A new RC program account will automatically be associated to the new BN . During the same phone call, follow step 3 .
Once the new BN has been registered with the CRA , any program accounts that are associated to your old BN , such as GST/HST (RT) program account or payroll (RP) program account, will not be automatically associated to the new BN . Therefore, you will have to:
- contact the CRA at 1-800-959-5525 to register for RT or RP program accounts on the new BN , if you need them for the new BN
- close your old BN and all the CRA program account associated to it

### When your business is no longer incorporated
Your legal status can change in one of the following ways:
- partnership to limited partnership
- corporation to sole proprietorship
- corporation to partnership
For these legal status changes, you have to call the CRA at 1-800-959-5525 .
When two or more corporations amalgamate, you must call the appropriate incorporating authority. For more information, go to Corporation income tax program account .
- About the deduction of Canada Pension Plan (CPP) contributions
- About the deduction of Employment Insurance (EI) premiums
- Business and industry
- Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
- Related provincial and territorial government sites
- Setting up your business
- When to file information returns

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
